mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the necessary components.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This command adds the Cluster API Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components into the specified namespace, which is required for managing Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

kubectl create namespace capi-system

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/capi --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

```

> Create a namespace for Cluster API components.
> Install Cluster API with the specified version.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/capi --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Provider
steps.description:
This step installs the necessary provider for your infrastructure (e.g., AWS, Azure). Replace 'aws' with your desired provider.

```bash

helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

```

> Install the AWS provider for Cluster API.
commands: helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

steps.title: Create a Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster Custom Resource (CR) to define the desired state of your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster
EOF

```

> This command creates a Cluster CR for AWS.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Cluster API components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> Verify that all Cluster API pods are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and may result in data loss if clusters are not deleted first. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace capi-system

kubectl delete namespace capi-system

```

> Uninstall the Cluster API components.
> Delete the namespace to clean up resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your cluster configurations. This step will upgrade the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o json > backup-clusters.json

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/capi --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> Backup existing cluster configurations.
> Upgrade Cluster API to the specified version.
> Verify that all Cluster API pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/capi --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: no provider found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the required provider is not installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the appropriate provider Helm chart is installed and configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This typically occurs when trying to interact with a cluster that has not been created or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Check the status of your Cluster Custom Resource and ensure it exists.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in capi-system namespace are in 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n capi-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the Kubernetes API server certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your kubeconfig is correctly configured and points to the right cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version for Cluster Custom Resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.12.0 or earlier, ensure to update your Cluster CR definitions to the new API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your cluster with the status 'Provisioned'.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/capi --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5
helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.5

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and have kubectl and helm installed.
